sql_array: CREATE TABLE sales (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, sale_date DATE, amount DECIMAL(10, 2), customer_id INTEGER);
CREATE TABLE customers (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR(50), signup_date DATE);
INSERT INTO customers VALUES (101, 'Alice', DATE '2022-09-15'), (102, 'Bob', DATE '2021-05-10');

SELECT c.name, SUM(s.amount) AS total_sales FROM customers c JOIN sales s ON c.id = s.customer_id WHERE s.sale_date > date_add(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL -1 MONTH) GROUP BY c.name HAVING total_sales > 50.00;
SELECT c.name, date_add(MIN(s.sale_date), INTERVAL (SUM(s.amount) / COUNT(s.id)) MONTH) AS predicted_next_sale_date FROM customers c JOIN sales s ON c.id = s.customer_id GROUP BY c.name;
SELECT customer_id, AVG(amount) AS average_sale, date_add(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL CASE WHEN COUNT(*) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END MONTH) AS next_possible_sale_date FROM sales GROUP BY customer_id;
SELECT c.id, c.name, date_add(MAX(s.sale_date), INTERVAL 5 DAY) AS next_sale_date FROM customers c LEFT JOIN sales s ON c.id = s.customer_id GROUP BY c.id, c.name HAVING next_sale_date IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, sale_date, amount + 0.05 AS adjusted_amount FROM sales WHERE sale_date < date_add(DATE '2023-01-31', INTERVAL 0 DAY) ORDER BY adjusted_amount DESC;

SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) AS sale_count FROM sales WHERE sale_date >= (SELECT date_add(MIN(sale_date), INTERVAL 3 MONTH) FROM sales WHERE customer_id IS NOT NULL) GROUP BY customer_id HAVING sale_count > 1;
